headline: Flood Damage Costs Kelantan RM4.7B Over 3 Consecutive Years
context: Annual monsoon floods caused cumulative economic damage of RM4.7 billion in Kelantan from 2023-2025, destroying crops, displacing 180,000 people, and wiping out small businesses that had not yet recovered from previous floods.

cards.big: RM4.7 billion in flood damage over 3 years — Kelantan loses 12% of state GDP to monsoons annually
cards.sub: Same communities flooded in 2023 were flooded again in 2024 and 2025 with no permanent mitigation built

cards.big: Agricultural losses of RM1.8 billion destroyed 42,000 hectares of rice, rubber, and tobacco crops
cards.sub: Farmers replant and lose again each cycle — insurance coverage reaches only 8% of Kelantan's agricultural output

cards.big: 180,000 displaced across 3 flood seasons — 12,000 families relocated more than 3 times without permanent resettlement
cards.sub: Mental health impact: 34% of repeatedly displaced residents show clinical anxiety symptoms per UKM study

cards.big: Federal flood mitigation allocation for Kelantan at RM1.2 billion remains only 28% disbursed after 4 years
cards.sub: Political tensions between PAS state government and federal Unity Government slow project approvals and releases

cards.big: Rebuilding the same roads and bridges every year isn't disaster recovery — it's a policy of planned destruction
Should flood mitigation funds be disbursed through an independent agency to remove state-federal political friction?
